name: Angular Patterns & Best Practices
description: Comprehensive guide to Angular component patterns, services, RxJS, state management, and performance optimization
version_note: Patterns focus on modern Angular (14+) with standalone components

feature_structure.description: Organize by feature, not by type
feature_structure.recommended_structure:
src/
  app/
    features/
      users/
        components/
        services/
        models/
        users.routes.ts
      products/
        components/
        services/
        models/
        products.routes.ts
    shared/
      components/
      directives/
      pipes/
    core/
      services/
      guards/
      interceptors/

input.description: Pass data from parent to child

multiple_slots.example:
// Component:
// Usage:

Title

Content

ng_template.example:
// Component: @Input() itemTemplate!: TemplateRef // Usage:
{{ item.name }}

default.description: Checks entire component tree on every event
default.performance: Can be slow for large apps

onpush.description: Only checks when inputs change or events occur

services.description: Business logic, data fetching, state management

provided_in.root: Singleton across app (recommended for most services)
provided_in.platform: Shared across apps in multi-app setup

multi_providers.description: Provide multiple values for same token

observables.creation:
const numbers$ = of(1, 2, 3)
const timer$ = interval(1000)

switchMap.when: HTTP requests, don't want to wait for previous

mergeMap.when: Independent requests that should run in parallel

concatMap.when: Order matters, wait for each to finish

shareReplay.use: Share and replay last N values

subjects.subject: Basic subject (no initial value)
subjects.behaviorSubject: Has current value, emits last value to new subscribers
subjects.replaySubject: Replays N last values to new subscribers

async_pipe.recommended: Use instead of manual subscribe in components

takeUntil_pattern.better: Use async pipe instead to avoid manual unsubscribe

reactive_forms.example:
form = new FormGroup({
  name: new FormControl('', [Validators.required]),
  email: new FormControl('', [Validators.required, Validators.email])
})

<form [formGroup]='form' (ngSubmit)='onSubmit()'>
  <input formControlName='name'>
  <input formControlName='email'>
  <button type='submit' [disabled]='form.invalid'>Submit</button>
</form>

benefits: Simpler than RxJS for simple state

trackBy.example:
{{ item.name }}
trackById(index: number, item: Item) { return item.id }

virtual_scrolling.example: {{ item }}

best_practices: Use standalone components for new projects
Use async pipe instead of manual subscribe
Use signals for simple reactive state (Angular 16+)
Use trackBy with ngFor for performance
Avoid DOM manipulation (use Renderer2 if needed)
Use virtual scrolling for long lists
Unsubscribe from observables (or use async pipe)
anti_patterns: Using default change detection for all components
